text.title: The ✨ultimate✨ road trip across Sicily | Scopello, Cefalu, Taormina, Ortigia

you play that song for me? It's my
favorite song.
[Music]
Let's
[Music]
go and down south. A smooth cat like me
got nothing to lose cuz I'm an outlaw.
Hey, listen. This town ain't enough for
the two of us. Come on now. If they want
that heat, just come through and eat it.
Everyone, we have successfully parked in
this little parking garage here. I know
it looks very sketchy, but it's cheap
and it's just a 4-minute walk from the
central market. And what the car rental
guy told us was that when you're parking
in Polarmo, you want to park in an
underground car park, you do not want to
be parking out on the street. So, yeah,
that was really comforting to know.
We're ready for some food. And honestly,
weather cannot be food.

Head over.

can get coffee. Wait on Roondo Beach.

and I won't think twice. Baby, will you

kind of just wandering around Scooello
because oh my god, what the heck?
This place is unreal. Seriously.
Anyways, we're waiting very patiently
for our hotel breakfast to open. What
the actual heck? Are you ready for this?
I don't think you're ready for this.

here with this view. See the sun slowly

night here because um the actual town
itself is very very very tiny.

just

the things you always

trying to find

going to find your dreams.

>> Now
all the saints

up
here.

all the
Let's go to Chaloo.
[Music]
All right, everyone. Welcome to our
Airbnb. We just finished chit cchatting
with our host, Francesco. He was so
nice. Even helped with my suitcase. What
a gentleman. Here's the entrance. And
then here we have the bedroom. And check
this out. Oh my goodness. I literally I
don't know why that impressed me so
much. I feel like that scene in Princess
Diaries when she's like pushing the limo
screen up and down. Anyways, moving on.
Here we've got a luxurious looking
bathroom with a view. Hello. While
you're doing your morning poo, you can
enjoy a beautiful little view. Adorable
little kitchen. And then we step outside
right here. This is the money maker,
people. And the best part about it is
that comes with free parking here for
two nights, 3 days. Anyways, we're going
to shower, refresh ourselves a little
bit, and then hit the town.
[Music]
As kind of our fourth little city/town
in Sicily, I think we were just
completely blown away with the landscape
of Sicily. This is the third little
beach town that we are at. So starting
with Scupello and then Pasta Lamar De
Gulo and then now Chefu. Honestly, just
the ambiance of these towns are just so
so so unbelievable. They're so quaint
and they're so quiet. I can only imagine
how insane it is during the summer.
We're here in October. It's just the
beginning of their autumn season. And
it's actually so beautiful. And our
Airbnb was just a short little 10-minute
walk from the old town. And just walking
through the old town is just so nice. I
mean, not a bad place to take a little
nap. And I think the key to Sicily is
not to have a crazy itinerary for each
of these towns. Just head out and you
explore. You just get lost and you just
discover so many little hidden gems, so
many beautiful places. And in my
opinion, that's so much better than just
having a little checklist to get
through.
I think that when it comes to food,
there are going to be hits and misses in
my opinion. We went to this restaurant
that was really highly recommended and
it's got lots of really really good
reviews on Google, but I don't know. We
kind of found it a little bit
underwhelming if I'm going to be super
honest. It was just okay. I don't think
it was like the best meal we've ever
had, but it was still a good meal. So,
who am I to complain?
[Music]
>> We have successfully found some eggs. We
are both loving chef at the moment.
[Music]
[Applause]
[Music]
[Music]
Stuck in the summer
and lost in the Good morning, Bonjono.
Everybody, we woke up a little bit early
this morning so that we can catch a cute
little sunrise run, but then I think we
spent a little bit too long on breakfast
and the sun has already risen. But
anyways, check it out.
How many KS do you want to run today?
>> I think it's right here.

>> These damn hills. I'm coming. It's like
I'm in the music.

this world

saporita. It's like meat and mushroom.

>> Mhm. It's so crunchy. And these were
only €3 each, by the way. I'm going to
try his I'm like double arenching it
right now.
M I genuinely don't think that you can
go wrong with the classic. It's so good.

D.
What if we could plan it out? Cover for
a week. Prepared for a day. We we can
come and be my babe. You could see what
a gentleman I am. Yeah. What if we could
plan it out? Party in the streets like
the golden days at the beach. family
buzzing in my air like a beat.
Maybe you could chill. All this stuff I
have up in my head, I just want to do.
Maybe if you come, then you can hang.
You could meet the crew. We could even
chill out at the park like we did in
school. Take you back to when you was
>> Hello everyone. We have resurrected from
the dead. We kind of decided to go back
to our Airbnb and chill out. We were
exhausted. It was getting actually quite
warm. The sun is intense here. Even
though it's like only 24°, cannot
imagine what it's actually like during
summer. It's only 5:00 and I am starving
and we have not we have not had a pizza
yet on this trip. And so it's our
mission tonight to get some pizza.
>> No options. If I finally tell the truth
that I'm telling it to you, what if we
could plan it out for a week,
pass for a day? We we you can come and
be
>> really nice with some chili oil. I'm not
going to lie.
>> What a gentleman I am. Yeah. What if we
could plan it out? Party in the streets.
>> Golden days at the beach. Family buzzing
in my air like a beat.
>> Maybe you could chill. I'm young.
Shy, you beneicent for real. Tell me how
it feels to be the one I meet. Hey, only
need a week with you, baby. You're so
bad. I cannot speak.
>> For our last day in Chef Lou, we decided
to go for another little early morning
run. And I don't know why this angle
actually. It's just so funny the way my
arms are pumping up and down. I look
like a little robot. But anyways, we did
another 5K early in the morning and it
was just such a peaceful, quiet little
morning. And then obviously we went back
to the Duomo Square and had another
little cappuccino plus a medso.
Honestly, we were so obsessed with these
things. They're just I don't know, so
light and airy. It's just basically a
bri stuffed with some cream. Amazing.
Then it was time to check out of our
beautiful Airbnb. And we were a little
bit sad to go, but very, very, very
excited for the next stop.
Goodbye, Chaffaloo.
Ciao for now.
[Music]
All righty, everybody. We are leaving
Chaloo and we're on route to Towerina. I
honestly think that Towerina is probably
the most hyped up town in Sicily. And
holy crap. Oh my god.
What?
What is this view? Are you kidding me?
Is stunning. Stunning.
me.
>> I really did like Chefaloo. I thought it
was a really, really, really cute little
town. The old town definitely worth
visiting. And now it's a 3hour and
54minut drive to Tower Mina. We're doing
the coastal route as you can see. In
terms of actually needing a car in
Sicily, I definitely think that you can
get away with just relying on the trains
because if you're only doing a few
towns, like the more popular ones, you
could definitely get away with just
doing the train. But because we wanted
to visit the smaller ones like Scopello,
there's no train to Scapello and it's
much much much quicker renting a car and
then you are able to actually, you know,
go at your own pace. You're not relying
on some sort of schedule. Towerina,
apparently the actual city center is a
ZTL zone, so you're not allowed to drive
into the city. You have to find paid
parking outside. Just also bear that in
mind. It is much more expensive to rent
a car. Obviously,
I think we have a bit of a problem here.
This is the road.
Thank you, Google Maps.
Okay, we made it out alive. This is like
the smallest little village.
[Music]
[Music]
It's really flipping cold right now.
>> Oh, this is
what this woods
keeper for me.
[Music]
My heart
[Music]
and sisters.
[Music]
Honestly, it was a really, really
convenient shuttle service and it got us
there in no time at all. And it was just
a short walk to our Airbnb where it was
not just one, not two, but three
separate stairwells to get to our actual
bedroom. And the views were just so
incredible. Honestly, I think at this
point in time, we were just so in awe.
[Music]
Definitely one of the more crowded
places that we've been so far. What
we've noticed is that in Sicily, every
town/ city has like a main street that's
like this. I will say right now that I
think that the one in Tower Mina is
probably the best. It's definitely the
most crowded and even with the crowds,
it's actually still very, very cute. And
here we have
Dio.
[Music]
Heat. Heat.
Heat.
[Music]
Heat.
[Music]
Mary,
[Music]
[Music]
[Applause]
>> it was such a beautiful performance. We
seriously loved it. I've never seen that
trio combination before. And so it was
just really, really cool. And then we
stumbled upon this shop that was so
stunning. It reminded me of a lot of the
shops that we have here in Melbourne.
And I seriously wanted this one pair of
shoes from there so bad, but they were
like €180, so we thought maybe not. And
then we ended the night with dessert
because of course you just have to. We
had some gelato and a canoli, which were
tbh, a little bit too sweet for my
liking, but still a good vibe.
[Music]
Lord,
[Music]
we are heading to breakfast now. We are
going to the iconic Bambar which is very
conveniently 2 minutes from my Airbnb.
So the agenda for today is we're going
to have breakfast at Bambar. This is
going to be one of the first times that
we are not having eggs for breakfast. So
then after breakfast we're going to go
to Isola Della. So as you can see we're
in our swimsuit and look it is blessedly
empty.
like this to take.
[Music]
I feel like you have to get the cream.
Anyways, I got the raspberry one.
[Music]
I have the exact same sentiment about
Tower Meta that I did with Chef Louu
what I mentioned before which is just
throw your itinerary out the window
genuinely maybe just have like one thing
on the agenda of like okay we're going
to go to this place but everything else
just explore
itself is not that massive and then when
you're walking through everything like
for example this garden was something
that I wanted to go to but we ended up
passing it anyways on our way to Isola
Bella and it was stunning. It was so
beautiful just wandering through it. I
absolutely loved it. And then we started
making our way down to Isolabella which
is one of the most iconic spots in
Towerina and I get why. Just a massive
disclaimer. You are going to have to
climb down and back up lots and lots of
steps. But it's absolutely worth it. And
you know what? Just come with that
mentality of okay, it's going to be a
workout to get there and it's going to
be a workout to come back from there.
And for us, because Tamina is so hilly,
we couldn't really go running slash we
didn't really want to go running. And so
this was just our form of exercise and
it was such a beautiful hike. Anyways,
one thing to know about Sicilian
beaches, at least the ones that we saw.
I'm sure that there are other beaches
that have sand, but these ones are very
rocky and they have rocks and stones
instead of sand. And so it's a little
bit painful on the feet. I'm not going
to lie, but it's still so beautiful.
Some of the best, most refreshing,
cleanest water I've ever swam in in my
life.
[Music]
We were all the way up there. Then we
climbed all the way down here. 10 out of
10. Would recommend.
[Music]
[Applause]
>> Baby can't float.
>> Yeah.
>> Yeah. Yeah.
We ended up staying for quite a bit of
time because this was the first proper
beach outing that we've done. We ended
up not going to the beach in Chefu and
getting into the water. I'm not entirely
sure why. I think we were just not
feeling it. But this beach in Taramina
and the water just looks so inviting. We
absolutely could not help ourselves. And
as soon as we got back, we were ready
ready for some lunch. And so we stopped
by this little fkatcha place, which is
actually a little bit pricier than I
expected it to be. I'm not going to lie.
But the fkacas were really really fresh
and tasty. and they were fat. We ended
up eating them on the steps in one of
the main squares before deciding to head
back up some more steps, but this time
we were climbing all the way up to the
Madonna Dear Rocka, which is this hill
that overlooks all of Tamina. The hike
itself is really not that bad. It was
very, very manageable and the views were
just so incredible. And again, it's just
a little bit more exercise. We
absolutely loved it. The weather was
weathering. It was absolutely stunning
that day. The sun was out in full force,
but it wasn't like overwhelmingly hot.
It was just maybe around like 25, 26
degrees that day, so it was perfection.
Then we went back to our Airbnb, took a
quick little shower, had another little
outfit change, did some wandering around
before heading to dinner. This was one
of the most popular pizza restaurants,
and it was so dang tasty. I honestly do
recommend it, but I am a sucker for
ambiance, and this restaurant had it.
The seating outside on the outdoor patio
was just beautiful. And even their
seating inside was also stunning as
well. I actually could not finish my
pizza. It was actually a lot of carbs
that day. And then we saw the most
stunning sunset of my life.
[Music]
Heat. Heat. Heat.
[Music]
free.
Good morning. We are at Bambar again as
you can see because I mean it was
delicious and it's our last day here.
So, but yes, we're going back to
Isolabella to do one last little swim,
one last little hike, and we have to
check out of our Airbnb at 10:00 a.m.
and then we're heading down to Syracuse.

Yeah.

There's heroes. We're shooting.

Okay, we have just arrived in Sakuza and
I'm not going to lie, it's not exactly
what I was expecting. Our Airbnb is kind
of located in Syracuse and I think the
cute part is in Artigia, which is like a
10-minute walk away. a little bit more
like commercial just it feels a little
bit rundown and the Airbnb that we
booked. I feel like they kind of
catfished us a little bit because they
said that it had a balcony and in the
photos the balcony pretty much overlooks
Ortigia and that's not what the view
from our balcony looks like at all.
That's okay. It's still nice. It's
comfy. This is where they shot the
movie. Yeah. I mean, sorry, the TV show.
This is the square that they danced,
which by the way is a great show. If you
have not seen that, the leopard is
probably one of the reasons why I said
yes to coming to Sicily because it was
beautiful.

Sus.

I sus.

Okay, Ortigia has grown on us. The
actual inside of it itself, it's so
quaint and beautiful and the streets are
clean and it's so so so like quiet and
it's actually beautiful. Lango Mari,
nice and peaceful. Stunning views.
[Applause]
Okay, we finally made it to dinner.
We're getting a mixed meat grill because
we need some protein.

picked up some groceries for brekie
tomorrow. I'm sorry. It's stunning here.

movie.

this is so pristine. Highly, highly,
highly recommend this spot. You guys, I
really like

our Airbnb and now we're going back to

to have a nice little cappuccino.
[Music]
We just bought this really cute print
from this local guy. He has set up his
like little booth on the side and look
how cute it is. He was so nice. I will
say I think the locals here in they seem
very very very sweet and just very kind
and friendly.
[Music]

We just got lunch.
It's massive. It's a one meter board.

are really good.

up
here.
Hello.
Thank you.
45minut drive later and we are here in
Mazi. According to Google only has like
360ish
residents.
It's like some sort of cyclist
convention.
It is very, very, very cute. I feel like
this place would 1,000% come alive in
the evening when they turn all the
lights on, which is what they kind of
really show in social media. It's very
small. I genuinely don't really know
besides the center what you could do
here.
[Music]
We are leaving Mortimei and we are going
to Nto
as is our experience with all these
little villages in pounds. The main
piaza, they light it up at night and
it's very, very, very cute. It's a short
31 minute drive, so we should arrive
there at 5:00 p.m. We've just finished
walking down the main villa. I just
really love it when all the towns light
up all their, you know, main streets
with all these like warm lights. Just
gives it such a nice cozy feel. I feel
like No would be cute for like a little
stop by like we've done. And now we're
heading off.
[Music]
[Music]
Oh my god,
that is everything I need right now. All
right, guys. I basically forgot to film
an outro. All I could concentrate on
honestly was just eating that panini,
which was so good. By the way, highly
recommend that spot if you're in Noto.
They were the kindest people ever. I
genuinely loved all the locals that we
met. I think that was part and parcel of
why our experience in Sicily was so
great. If I could sum up our experience
in Sicily, it was magnificent. So, going
in October, I actually really, really
loved because there weren't too many
crowds. It wasn't too hot. It has more
of like a local charm to it. That was
the feeling that we got. In any case,
would we go back? Absolutely. I think
our favorite even though it might be
expected and a little bit basic, but Sue
West Towermina is popular for a reason.
And out of all the places that we
visited, it was definitely our favorite
in terms of the vibe, the culture,
things to do, and just the sites and the
views and everything like that. I would
love to encourage you to visit Sicily if
you can one day. Put it on your bucket
list because it is so worth it.
